body:
MILESTONE 2: regime classification + Bayesian belief system reviewed. This directly answers two items from your reinforcement checklist:

REGIME TRANSITIONS -- GOOD, already validated properly: ClassifyBarToRegime_WithHysteresis() requires structural confirmation (RegimeHasStructuralConfirmation) AND a minimum confirmed-bar count before committing a regime change, with the required bar count itself adjusted by purity/certainty (ambiguous readings need more confirmation, high-certainty readings need less). It also has an ADX-persistence filter that resists flipping out of a strong trend on noise. This is real hysteresis, not an instant snap-to-new-regime. No fix needed here.

BAYESIAN BELIEF -- FOUND THE EXACT GAP YOU ASKED ME TO CHECK FOR: the 'Bayesian regime belief' block (~line 30745) reinitializes to a UNIFORM PRIOR (1/9 each) on every single call, then multiplies by ONLY the current tick's instantaneous evidence (ADX, Hurst, RSI, body%, volume, structure flags -- all current-bar readings). There's no persistence of the previous posterior into the next prior. That means this isn't actually a Bayesian *update* -- it's a fresh memoryless likelihood snapshot recomputed from scratch every call, so it snaps to single-tick evidence exactly as your checklist worried about. This ISN'T just diagnostic -- I traced its consumers: dominantBeliefRegime and beliefConviction feed into real threshold-relaxation logic (line 7822: 'if beliefConviction > 0.70 && dominantBeliefRegime == regime' loosens requirements) and another consumer at line 14787. So a single noisy bar can currently swing a live threshold adjustment. Fix: carry the previous _bel[] forward as the next call's prior (with a decay/forgetting factor rather than a hard reset) so belief actually accumulates across bars instead of resetting every tick. This is a real, valuable fix to make, not a rewrite -- the evidence-likelihood math per regime is already reasonable, it just needs to stop discarding its own history.

BONUS STRUCTURAL FINDING (resolves the 9-vs-12 regime question from milestone 1): the ACTUAL classifier is unambiguously 9-state -- I traced it to source: ClassifyBarToRegime_WithHysteresis loops 'for(_ri=0;_ri<9;_ri++) // 9 regimes', the belief array is regimeBelief[9], and g_RegimeState.currentRegime is only ever assigned from rawRegime = ClassifyBarToRegime(...) which is bounded to 0-8. But regimeStrategies[9], [10], [11] are fully populated with real, distinct, deliberately-tuned values (position sizing, weights, TP/SL, risk tolerance -- not placeholder zeros) that can never be reached by any live trade, because nothing in the classifier can ever emit regime 9, 10, or 11. My guess: these were built for the 'adaptive/hybrid regime' concept mentioned in the Layer 1 Creative Adaptation comments (useAdaptiveRegime) but that wiring was never finished. Question for you rather than something I'll decide unilaterally: do you want these 3 slots (a) wired up to something real, (b) deleted as dead config, or (c) left alone for now? I'll default to (c) -- leave them untouched -- unless you say otherwise, since deleting tuned values or guessing at wiring intent on a live-adjacent system isn't my call to make alone.

Continuing into OnInit/OnTick next.
